claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and the child or children you're inquiring about.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have a child (or children) that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you please tell me:
- How many children you're asking about?
- Their names (or you can use Child 1, Child 2, etc. if you prefer)?
- Their ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child I'm responsible for. I have one child - Alex, who is 18 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I can see that Alex is 18 years old.

Since Alex is over 16, I need to ask some follow-up questions to determine eligibility:

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** 

This would typically include things like:
- A-levels or similar qualifications
- T-levels
- Scottish Highers
- NVQs up to level 3
- Home education (if started before they turned 16)
- Certain types of approved training

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is still in education - studying for A-levels at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's helpful to know.

Since Alex is 18 and in approved education (A-levels), I need to ask:

**When did Alex turn 18, or when will Alex turn 19?** 

This is important because Child Benefit can continue after age 16 if the child is in approved education or training, but it usually stops on specific dates related to their birthday and when they leave education.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex turned 18 a few months ago - still well under 19, so not approaching that age yet.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. A few more questions to complete the assessment:

**When does Alex's A-level course end?** Is it expected to finish:
- Before the 31st of August (the end of this current academic year)?
- After the 31st of August?

And also, **has Alex left the A-level course, or is Alex still actively enrolled and attending?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is still actively enrolled and attending. The A-levels are expected to finish at the end of the academic year, so before the 31st of August.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I need to understand a bit more about your own circumstances:

**Are you the person who is primarily responsible for Alex?** In other words:
- Does Alex live with you?
- Are you the main person responsible for Alex's care and upbringing?

And if there's another parent or guardian involved, do they also live in the same household, or does Alex live with you alone/primarily with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me and I'm the main person responsible for their care. It's just the two of us in the household.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Do you normally live in the UK?** (This means the UK is your main home where you usually reside)

And **are you subject to immigration control** (meaning do you have any restrictions on your immigration status that would prevent you from claiming public funds)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - this is my main home. And no, I'm not subject to any immigration control or restrictions.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for providing all that information. Let me just confirm one final detail:

**Has Alex enrolled on, been accepted for, or started any further education or training that will continue after the 31st of August?** For example:
- University
- Apprenticeship
- Another college course
- Any other education or training programme

This is important because Child Benefit typically stops at a certain point when young people finish their school-level education.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex hasn't enrolled in anything yet for after August. Still figuring out what to do next after the A-levels finish.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Good News - You Are Eligible! ✓

Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

### Here's why:

**For Alex:**
- Alex is 18 years old and still in approved education (A-levels)
- Under UK Child Benefit rules, you can continue to receive Child Benefit for a child aged 16-19 if they are in approved education or training
- A-levels qualify as approved education
- Alex is actively enrolled and attending the course

**Your circumstances:**
- You live in the UK and it's your main home ✓
- You're not subject to immigration control ✓
- Alex lives with you ✓
- You're the main person responsible for Alex's care ✓

### Important Information:

Your Child Benefit for Alex will continue until **31st August** this year (when the A-level course ends), or until the 31st of August following Alex's 19th birthday - whichever comes first.

Since Alex's A-levels finish before the 31st of August this year and Alex hasn't enrolled in any further education or training after that date, your Child Benefit will stop at the end of August when the course ends.

If Alex decides to enroll in further approved education or training (like university or another qualifying course) that starts in September, you may be able to continue claiming until the later date, so it's worth keeping that in mind.

Do you have any questions about your eligibility or what happens next?

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and still in approved education (A-levels). Under UK Child Benefit rules, you can continue to receive Child Benefit for a child aged 16-19 if they are in approved education or training. Alex is actively enrolled and attending A-levels, which qualifies as approved education. The benefit will continue until the 31st of August following Alex's 19th birthday, or until Alex leaves approved education, whichever comes first. Since Alex turned 18 only a few months ago and the A-level course finishes before the 31st of August this year, you remain eligible for Child Benefit for Alex during this period. The claimant lives in the UK, is not subject to immigration control, Alex lives with them, and they are the main person responsible for Alex's care, meeting all the residency and responsibility requirements.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. Alex meets the age and education criteria (18 years old and in approved A-level education), and the claimant meets all the residency and responsibility requirements as they live in the UK, are not subject to immigration control, and Alex lives with them as the main carer. The benefit will continue until Alex finishes the A-levels (before 31st August) or until the 31st of August following Alex's 19th birthday, whichever comes first.
